prompt: The lambda does not compile. What is the bug?
choices: Function requires the return type to be declared in the lambda
Function<String, String> has a single String input and String output; the lambda takes two parameters (String and int) which does not match; use BiFunction<String, Integer, String> instead
Lambda parameters must not declare types when used with Function
repeat is not defined on String in this version of Java
explanation: Function<T, R> represents a function with one input (T) and one output (R). A lambda with two parameters (String, int) does not satisfy Function<String,String>'s single-argument apply method. Fix: BiFunction<String, Integer, String> fn = (s, n) -> s.repeat(n);
